parameters.content:
## Trigger Node
- This automation will run when the user types 'Run'

- This form has two selections: how many data, and sort by

## Logic Node
- Check if the user type "Run" if yes get the data in sheet, if no, stop the workflow.

## Action Node
- Get the data from List tab, and only retrieve active one

- If there's multiple active status, get only one, the first one, LIMIT to one

## Action Node
- Get popular tags from steamDB

## Data Transform Node
- Get the data from the previous node ("limit"), match the tags with the previous node that gets popular tags, and return the results if there is a match.

## Logic Node
- If the **tag ID** is not null, it means there is a **match**. If the **tag ID** is null, it means there is **no match**.

## Output Node - Save
- Update list tabs of that steam_tags to error if no matching tags found

parameters.name: User-Agent
parameters.value: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

nodes.notes: Use the default endpoint of SteamDB related to Steam game tags, implement pagination, pass the user's “how many data” selection, and implement a random delay.

HR: GET APPID FROM LOGO
nodes.notes: Extract the app ID from the logo field to return a proper name and app ID format.

parameters.name: User-Agent
parameters.value: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

nodes.name: HR: GET INFO PER APPID
nodes.notes: Get the information related to that appid and implement batching of 10 items per randomize delay

nodes.name: HR: GET GAME NAME, APPID, PUBLISHER, DEVELOPER
nodes.notes: Fixed the format of previous node, only retrieve the game name, app id, publisher, and developer name

nodes.name: HR: GET DATA FROM USER TAG NODE
nodes.notes: Get the data from HR: WAIT - USER TAG to match with previous node, we will use the matching to update the List tab

parameters.query:
SELECT id, name, visibility, NULL AS status
FROM (
  SELECT id, name, visibility
  FROM public.catalogue_products
  WHERE name LIKE $${{ $json.game_name }}$$
  LIMIT 1
) t

UNION ALL

SELECT 
  NULL AS id,
  $${{ $json.game_name }}$$ AS name,
  NULL AS visibility,
  'game name is not found in madmin' AS status
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 
  FROM public.catalogue_products 
  WHERE name LIKE $${{ $json.game_name }}$$
);

nodes.name: HR: CHECK IF GAME EXIST IN MADMIN
nodes.notes: Check if the game from steam exist on Madmin, check in catalogue_products table, If exist, return the id, name, visibility(check if hidden or not)

nodes.name: HR: FORMAT MADMIN OUTPUT V1
nodes.notes: Fix the format of Madmin, return the product id, game name, convert the visibility if 0 meaning hidden, if 1 meaning not hidden, and provide product link

parameters.query:
WITH product_check AS (
    SELECT 
        cp.id AS product_id,
        cp.name AS product_name,
        cp.has_organisation
    FROM public.catalogue_products cp
    WHERE cp.name LIKE $${{ $json.madmin_game_name }}$$
    LIMIT 1
),

organisation_lookup AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        po.id,
        po.name,
        po.segment_id
    FROM product_check pc

    JOIN public.catalogue_product_meta_data cpm
        ON pc.product_id = cpm.product_id

    JOIN public.catalogue_meta_data cmd
        ON cpm.meta_data_id = cmd.id

    JOIN public.publisher_organisations po
        ON cmd.publisher_organisation_id = po.id
        AND po.active = true

    WHERE pc.has_organisation = true

    LIMIT 1
),

contact_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        pm.organisation_id,
        COUNT(DISTINCT pu.id) AS contact_count
    FROM public.publisher_memberships pm

    JOIN public.publisher_users pu
        ON pm.user_id = pu.id
         AND pu.email NOT IN (
        'proxy.admin@keymailer.co',
        'admin@keymailer.co'
    )

    GROUP BY pm.organisation_id
)

SELECT 
    pc.product_name,
    ol.id AS organisation_id,
    ol.name AS organisation_name,
    ol.segment_id,
    COALESCE(cc.contact_count, 0) AS contact_count,
    CASE 
        WHEN pc.has_organisation = false THEN 'no active organisation found'
        WHEN ol.id IS NULL THEN 'no active organisation found'
        ELSE 'active organisation found'
    END AS status
FROM product_check pc
LEFT JOIN organisation_lookup ol ON true
LEFT JOIN contact_counts cc ON ol.id = cc.organisation_id;

nodes.name: HR: CHECK GAME PUBLISHER IN MADMIN
nodes.notes: Check if the game name has an active publisher in madmin, if yes return it with how many users it has, do not include the users who has proxy.admin@keymailer.co and admin@keymailer.co

nodes.name: HR: FORMAT THE MADMIN OUTPUT V2
nodes.notes: Fix the format of previous node, return the product name, publisher id and name, publisher link, publisher link, segment, contact count

nodes.name: HR: MERGE ALL DATA
nodes.notes: Get the data from user tag, loop, format madmin output v1 and previous node, use that to merge all data gathered and save the results of steam and madmin together

nodes.name: HR: GET THE DATA FROM OUTPUT V2 NODE
nodes.notes: Get the data from FORMAT THE MADMIN OUTPUT V2, we will use that to check the publisher users

parameters.query:
SELECT
    po.name AS organisation_name,
    pu.first_name,
    pu.last_name,
    pu.email AS email_address,
    pm.role,
    pu.current_sign_in_at
FROM public.publisher_organisations po
JOIN public.publisher_memberships pm
    ON po.id = pm.organisation_id
JOIN public.publisher_users pu
    ON pm.user_id = pu.id
WHERE po.id = {{$json.madmin_publisher_id}}
    AND pu.deleted_at IS NULL
    AND pu.email NOT IN (
        'proxy.admin@keymailer.co',
        'admin@keymailer.co'
    )
ORDER BY pu.first_name, pu.last_name
LIMIT 100;

nodes.name: HR: CHECK PUBLISHER USERS
nodes.notes: Get the publisher users, return first name, last name, email, role, and current login

parameters.content:
## Event Promo

**Purpose:**
- The purpose of this workflow is to get search tags in steamdb, get the games of that tags, cross-reference in madmin, check if the game exist in madmin, get the publisher and publisher users

## Documentation
- This workflow will run when the user types Run in the form.

- In the form, there are one optional field:
    1. How many data - how many data you want to return, 100 items, 200 items, 300 items

- Get all the games related to steam tags

- Get the information of that game in steam such as publisher, developer, and game link

- Check if the game found in steam exist on Madmin, If no stop

- If yes, check if there's an publisher, if yes, get the publisher user

- Save the results
